meta.title: End Behavior of -x¹⁰ - 3x⁹ + x² | MAT 170 Exam 2

steps.narration: Use the leading coefficient test for f of x equals negative x to the tenth minus 3 x to the ninth plus x squared.
steps.label: MAT 170 Exam 2 Review

steps.narration: The leading term is negative x to the tenth.
The degree is 10, which is even. That means both ends go in the same direction.
The leading coefficient is negative 1. Negative with even degree means: both ends fall.
As x goes to negative infinity, f of x goes to negative infinity.
As x goes to positive infinity, f of x also goes to negative infinity. Both ends point down.
Think of it like an upside-down parabola shape at the extremes. Both ends fall.
steps.label: Summary
